name: Monte de Hijedo — circular desde Arija
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Arija offer a diverse landscape characterized by the expansive Ebro Reservoir, pine forests, and sandy dunes. The region features varied terrain, including dense beech and oak woods, providing a range of natural backdrops for rides. Riders can explore paths along the reservoir's edge and through forested areas, with elevation changes typical of the area. This blend of water features and woodlands creates a compelling environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Arija The most popular mountain bike trail is Pico Jano – circular desde Lanchares por el embalse de Alsa, a 31.6 miles (50.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Monte de Hijedo — circular desde Arija, a difficult 20.8 miles (33.5 km) path. This route explores the Monte de Hijedo area, offering a challenging ride through natural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the El Mediano Frío Viewpoint – Alsa Reservoir loop from Centro Ornitológico del Embalse del Ebro, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail leading through the Alsa Reservoir area, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Mountain biking around Arija is defined by the Ebro Reservoir, dense pine forests, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes are challenging. The routes in Arija are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Arija's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Arija?
A: Arija features 6 mountain bike trails, offering a variety of experiences for riders. While many are challenging, the diverse landscape provides engaging rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Arija?
A: The terrain around Arija is quite varied. You'll encounter paths along the expansive Ebro Reservoir, ride through dense pine forests and sandy dunes, and explore areas with beech and oak woods. Expect elevation changes typical of the region, providing a compelling environment for mountain biking.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy mountain bike trails in Arija?
A: While many of the listed komoot routes are rated as difficult, the region research mentions the 'Playa de Arija Mountain Biking Route' as an 8-mile easy trail suitable for various skill levels, featuring sandy dunes and pine forests with scenic views of the Ebro reservoir. This route can be ridden in both directions.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Arija?
A: Yes, several routes in Arija are circular. For example, you can try &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999831293/pico-jano-circular-desde-lanchares-por-el-embalse-de-alsa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jano – circular desde Lanchares por el embalse de Alsa&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 31.6-mile route, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100735116/monte-de-hijedo-circular-desde-arija" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de Hijedo — circular desde Arija&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 20.8-mile path exploring the Monte de Hijedo area.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking in Arija?
A: The Ebro Reservoir is a central feature, offering stunning views. You might also encounter the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5378470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of the Church of San Roque (The Cathedral of the Fish)&lt;/a&gt;, which rises above the reservoir waters. The trails also pass through diverse landscapes including pine, beech, and oak forests, and sandy dunes near the 'Playa de Arija'.

Q: Are there any historical sites or interesting places to explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, you can explore several interesting sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5346281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hijedo Cabin&lt;/a&gt; is a historical site. The charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corconte&lt;/a&gt;, located on the shore of the Ebro reservoir, is known for its natural water sources and historical architecture. You might also find old washing basins and fountains built of ashlar, adding historical charm to the natural surroundings.

Q: What is the difficulty level of mountain biking in Arija?
A: Mountain biking in Arija tends to be challenging. Out of the 6 available routes, 5 are rated as difficult, and 1 is moderate. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain, suitable for riders with good fitness.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Arija?
A: The komoot community rates the trails in Arija highly, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. Riders often appreciate the diverse landscapes, including the Ebro Reservoir, dense pine forests, and varied elevation changes that define the area's mountain biking experience.

Q: Is there a route that offers good viewpoints?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Mediano Frío Viewpoint – Alsa Reservoir loop from Centro Ornitológico del Embalse del Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a 25.3-mile difficult trail that leads through the Alsa Reservoir area, likely offering scenic vistas.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through forested areas?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Arija traverse forested landscapes. For instance, the 'Playa de Arija Mountain Biking Route' takes riders through picturesque pine forests, and other areas near Quintanilla de Santa Gadea feature dense beech and oak woods, providing shaded and scenic sections.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Monte de Hijedo area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100735116/monte-de-hijedo-circular-desde-arija" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de Hijedo — circular desde Arija&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 20.8-mile path specifically designed to explore the Monte de Hijedo area, offering a challenging ride through its natural landscapes.
tips: Path to the top of Monte de Hijedo surrounded by dense beech and oak forests that invite you to explore and enjoy the beauty of nature.

Located in the heart of the beautiful Mount Hijedo, the Cabin is a small palace with typical regional architecture that was built at the beginning of the 20th century and next to the main building is the small hermitage.
Extraordinary views of the Robla train bridge with its spectacular arches and the beautiful panorama of the Ebro reservoir in the background.

waypoints.name: View of the Robla Train Bridge
